job_description:
At ST, we believe in the power of technology to drive innovation and make a positive impact on people, business, and society. We are a global semiconductor company, and our advanced technology & chips forms the hidden part of the world we live in today.

When you join ST, you will be part of a global business of more than 115+ nationalities and present in 40 countries, 50,000+, diverse and dedicated creators & makers of technology around the world!

Developing technologies takes more than talent: it takes amazing people who understands collaboration and respect. People with passion and desire to disrupt the status quo, push boundaries and drive innovation – whilst unlocking your own potential.

Working at ST means innovating for a future that we want to make smarter, greener, in a responsible and sustainable way. Our technology starts with you. Join us and start the future!

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Data Analytics Intern to join our AME Strategic Marketing team. This intern will play a crucial role in analyzing semiconductor marketshare data, aggregating information from various internal and external databases, and developing insightful reports and dashboards using Microsoft Power BI. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in data analysis, a foundational understanding of database concepts, and a desire to learn about the dynamic semiconductor industry.

Key Responsibilities

Data Collection & Integration: Assist in identifying, collecting, and integrating semiconductor market share data from diverse sources, including internal sales databases, external market research reports, and public datasets.
Data Cleaning & Transformation: Support the cleaning, validation, and transformation of raw data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and usability for analysis.
Database Aggregation: Work with existing database structures (e.g., SQL Server, Excel, CSVs) to aggregate and consolidate relevant marketshare information.
Power BI Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports in Microsoft Power BI to visualize semiconductor market trends, competitive landscapes, and marketshare shifts.
Data Analysis: Conduct preliminary data analysis to identify key trends, patterns, and anomalies within the semiconductor market.
Reporting & Presentation: Contribute to the preparation of regular marketshare reports and presentations for internal stakeholders.
Documentation: Document data sources, data transformation processes, and Power BI dashboard logic.
Ad-hoc Support: Provide support for ad-hoc data requests and analytical projects as needed.

Qualifications

Education: Currently enrolled in Master's degree program in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Technical Skills:
Basic understanding of relational databases and SQL.
Familiarity with Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, data manipulation).
Exposure to Microsoft Power BI (dashboard creation, DAX).
Basic programming skills (e.g., Python for data manipulation) are a plus but not required.
Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with an aptitude for working with large datasets.
Attention to Detail: High level of accuracy and attention to detail in data handling and reporting.
Communication: Good verbal and written communication skills.
Initiative: Proactive, self-starter with a strong desire to learn and contribute.
Industry Interest: Demonstrated interest in the semiconductor industry and market dynamics.
Learning Opportunities

Gain hands-on experience with real-world semiconductor market data.
Develop expertise in Power BI for business intelligence and data visualization.
Enhance SQL skills and learn about database integration strategies.
Understand the intricacies of market share analysis and competitive intelligence.
Work closely with experienced data analysts and business stakeholders.
Opportunity to contribute to impactful business decisions.

We encourage candidates who may not meet every single requirement to apply, as we appreciate diverse perspectives and provide opportunities for growth and learning. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) is part of our company culture. Our DEI vision is, “At ST, you can be the true version of yourself”, we value all employee contributions and have zero tolerance for any kind of discrimination.

Joining us is also about a greater work-life balance and workplace with equal opportunities. Dedicated Employee Resource Groups for women and LGBTQIA+, hybrid work arrangements are amongst the many DEI & Sustainability initiatives that make us a great place to evolve your career.

selection_logic: Industry is selected before role so the user can ride stronger policy and market tailwinds.
Scoring combines macro attractiveness, current skill fit, entry friendliness, and visa friction.
Top industries should feed company strategy before final role positioning.

discovery_strategy: Use retrieval to find industry-matched companies before asking the model to summarize them.
Prioritize A/B-stage signals when the provider has stage data; otherwise prefer growth-stage companies over mature incumbents.
Bias toward regions and operating models that fit the user's mobility constraints.
target_company_types: Healthcare analytics and data infrastructure vendors
company_selection_rules: Prioritize companies where data work influences revenue, product adoption, or operational efficiency.
Prefer teams where analytics sits close to decision-makers rather than isolated reporting functions.
Use industry tailwinds to narrow company types before narrowing exact job titles.
Favor companies with clear analyst-to-strategic-role progression and measurable first-project scope.
Add screening for international hiring maturity, remote flexibility, and globally distributed teams.
ranking_logic: Industry alignment is the base score because company discovery starts from the chosen sectors.
Company stage gets extra weight, with a preference for A/B-stage signals and then growth-stage firms.
Region fit, remote flexibility, and hiring signal quality help break ties.
industry_analysis: Healthcare AI rewards companies that connect model performance to clinical workflow adoption.
Data access, regulatory trust, and integration depth are stronger moats than generic model novelty.
Enterprise SaaS gives broad access to product, revenue, and usage data with relatively clear experimentation loops.
AI-native workflow software creates strong openings for analytics-driven product strategy roles.
Fintech companies create strong data roles where analytics links directly to growth, fraud, and retention.
The highest leverage teams sit close to risk, monetization, and operational automation.
market_analysis: Hospitals and providers buy cautiously, so workflow ROI and trust are critical.
Platform businesses that embed into existing systems often scale better than point tools.
B2B buyers now expect AI augmentation and measurable productivity gains.
Companies with product-led data loops often develop stronger analyst and data scientist scope.
Efficiency-focused buyers reward products that reduce manual operations and trust failures.
Infrastructure and B2B fintech often provide clearer data ownership than consumer-only products.
value_chain_analysis: High-value positions sit near clinical data infrastructure, documentation workflows, and decision support.
The best operating leverage comes from turning fragmented healthcare processes into repeatable data products.
The most strategic teams sit between product usage, monetization, and go-to-market efficiency.
Observability, collaboration, and workflow software each create strong instrumentation surfaces.
Payments rails, underwriting, and finance automation are strong analytics-heavy layers.
Companies with both product telemetry and transaction data tend to create richer role scope.
competitor_map: Clinical AI platforms: Tempus, Abridge, Suki
Data and oncology infrastructure: Flatiron Health, Guardant Health
Observability and infrastructure: Datadog, New Relic
Collaboration and workflow: Notion, Asana, Airtable

Build stakeholder understanding around clinical documentation and workflow ai.
Ship one small but visible analysis, dashboard, or workflow improvement that proves execution speed.
Translate job requirements into a 30-day proof-of-value checklist.
month_2_3_plan: Own a recurring analysis or decision-support workflow instead of one-off reporting.
Develop domain fluency so recommendations reflect industry economics, not just data outputs.
Turn one technical capability into a reusable artifact such as a dashboard, model, template, or playbook.
Strengthen cross-functional trust by connecting analysis to a business decision or operational win.
one_year_plan: Become the go-to operator for data analyst problems.
Build a portfolio of impact stories that compound into promotion or stronger external positioning.
Expand from execution into prioritization by influencing what should be measured, automated, or changed.
Create option value for the next move across stronger roles, better companies, or deeper domain specialization.
daily_skill_accumulation: Spend 30-45 minutes strengthening one role-critical skill with a concrete artifact every day.
Maintain a running evidence log of business impact, stakeholder wins, and domain insights.
Read one company, industry, or market note daily to stay macro-aware instead of tool-focused.
Refine one resume or narrative bullet each week using fresh evidence from projects.
value_creation_plan: Target value creation where Healthcare AI has active tailwinds.
Focus on problems close to clinical documentation and workflow ai.
Prefer work that reduces decision latency, improves forecast quality, or creates reusable operational leverage.
Document outcomes in a way that can later power interviews, resumes, and promotion cases.
